div.head: The Chargé in Germany (Heath) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
2036. At approximately 10:30 tonight the German radio broadcast a special bulletin announcing the signature at 6:50 this evening in the forest of Compiegne of a Franco-German armistice agreement.Department of State, Documents on German Foreign Policy, 1918–1945, series D, Vol. ix, p. 671. At the same time it was stated that French plenipotentiaries would proceed to a meeting with Italian plenipotentiaries and that the Franco-German armistice agreement would not enter into effect until 6 hours after the signature of an Italian-French armistice agreement. After
the singing of national anthems the announcement concluded with the declaration that now the struggle goes forward against England.
The bulletin contained no information concerning the terms of the Franco-German armistice agreement.
